user_gap: KDT OS detected Health Monitoring in this uploaded project and created a ready practice quest.
resume_rule: After this quest is complete, return to the original project or goal that caused this quest unless you intentionally choose a different path.
title: Mini Health Checker
goal: Practice health monitoring by checking a tiny system and reporting whether it looks OK.
what_to_build: A Python command-line app that checks three fake or simple health values and prints a clear OK/WARNING report.
requirements: Create three checks: cpu_check, memory_check, and disk_check
Each check must return OK or WARNING
Create README.md explaining how to run it
success_criteria: At least three health checks exist
Output clearly shows OK or WARNING
The project can be uploaded as proof
steps: Create a new folder named mini_health_checker
Inside app.py, create a function named cpu_check() that returns OK
Create a main section that calls all three functions
Print each result on its own line
Print a final line that says Overall Health: Needs Attention if any check is WARNING
Create README.md with the exact command: python app.py
Zip the folder and submit it
proof_required: Include screenshot or copied terminal output showing OK/WARNING
Answer: what makes a check become WARNING?
why_this_exists: This replaces an older quest that looked too generic. KDT OS regenerated it using the skill (Health Monitoring), source (Project Analysis: FieldSeed), and your need for exact, verifiable steps.

teach_me_breakdown.label: What is this skill?
teach_me_breakdown.answer: Health Monitoring is a skill KDT OS can practice through small exact quests.

teach_me_breakdown.label: Why practice it?
teach_me_breakdown.answer: Practicing it in a tiny project makes it easier to use later in a larger project.
